summary: sensivity ships obfuscated launcher executing PowerShell/VBScript with network exfiltration primitives
details: The package contains multiple components consistent with a multi-stage Windows malware loader. cli.js imports child_process and uses http.get together with shell enumeration commands (ls, ping, environment access). launcher.js spawns and execSyncs PowerShell (execSync('powershell...'), spawn('powershell...')) and references wscript with a.vbs payload — the classic LOLBin chain for executing scripted droppers on Windows. server.obf.js is a heavily obfuscated bundle (hex-mangled identifiers like _0x2f6402, _0x251406, _0x5a3aeb), hiding its behavior from static review. The combination of obfuscated server code, a launcher that invokes PowerShell and Windows Script Host, and a CLI module that pairs child_process with HTTP and host reconnaissance is not consistent with any legitimate purpose and matches the structural fingerprint of a credential/host-data stealer with remote payload execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
